ety: inf. n. iv of حسن 'to be or become good,' &c.
def: v.t. To upbraid (one) with favours conferred, to cast obligations in the teeth of; to speak or boast of kindnesses or benefits conferred
more: (inf. iv. of حسن) s. m. Beneficence, benevolence, benevolent actions, benefit, favour, courtesy, kindness, the conferring of obligation. احسان کرنا iḥsān karnā, To confer favour or benefit, to oblige. احسانمند iḥsān-mand, adj. Obliged, bound (by favour), grateful.
subs: (-par), To confer a favour (upon); to place (one) under an obligation
adj. Forgetful of benefits or favours; ungrateful, thankless
s.m. Requital or return for a favour; grateful acknowledgment
(-par), To confer a favour or benefit (upon), to oblige, to benefit; to do a kind act, or a good turn (to)
(se), To accept or receive a favour (from); to be under obligation (to)
(-kā), To acknowledge or be grateful for kindness, &c. (of or from); to feel obliged, feel grateful
s.f. Gratefulness, thankfulness; a sense of obligation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian احسان (ihsān), from Arabic إِحْسَان (ʔiḥsān).

def: s.m. (pl. of ěḥsān), Favours, kindnesses, benefits, good offices, &c.

adj. Ped. Ungrateful; thankless. ehsān kā badlā , Requital or return for any favor; a return. ehsān kar'nā , v. a. To confer a favor or benefit; to oblige; to do a good turn or service; to serve; benefit. ehsān le'nā , v. n. To be indebted to; to be under an obligation; to receive a favor. ehsān' mān'nā , v. n. To feel obliged; to be grateful or indebted to. Khudā kā to ehsān māntā hī nahīṅ, hamārā kab mānegā?

ety: inf. n. iv of حسن 'to be or become good,' &c.
def: v.t. To upbraid (one) with favours conferred, to cast obligations in the teeth of; to speak or boast of kindnesses or benefits conferred
subs: (-par), To confer a favour (upon); to place (one) under an obligation
adj. Forgetful of benefits or favours; ungrateful, thankless
s.m. Requital or return for a favour; grateful acknowledgment
(-par), To confer a favour or benefit (upon), to oblige, to benefit; to do a kind act, or a good turn (to)
(se), To accept or receive a favour (from); to be under obligation (to)
(-kā), To acknowledge or be grateful for kindness, &c. (of or from); to feel obliged, feel grateful
s.f. Gratefulness, thankfulness; a sense of obligation.

def: adj. Thankful; grateful; obliged.
adj. v.s.v. احسان ěḥsān.
n. f. Gratitude; a sense of obligation; thankfulness. ehsān hai , intj. 1. Thanks! many thanks! much obliged! 2. Thank Heaven! Heaven be praised! Khudā kā ehsān hai!

ety: inf. n. viii of حکر, 'To act wrongfully'
def: s.m. Collecting and hoarding up grain, &c. in expectation of its becoming dear.

ety: inf. n. viii of حلم
def: s.m. Emission of the seminal fluid in dreaming; nocturnal pollution.
more: n. m. Ped. for Pop. Mah. nahāne kī hājit , Wom. Shaitānī (Devil's act). Wet dreams; nocturnal pollution.
(inf. viii. of حلم Heb. and Ch. He dreamt) s. m. Nocturnal pollution.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِحْتِلَام (ihtilām), from Arabic اِحْتِلَام (iḥtilām).

ety: inf. n. viii of حمل 'to bear'
def: s.m. Supposition, hypothesis; admissibleness, allowableness; probability; likelihood; presumption; uncertainty, doubt; attributing; imputation
more: n. m. حَمَلَ he bore. 1. Reasonable chance; fair chance; probability; likelihood; presumption ( bharam ). 2. Conjecture; guess; supposition ( aṭkal ). 3. Doubt ( shubah ); suspense ( dubdhā ); uncertainty.

(inf. viii. of حمل Heb.and Syr. He bore) s. m. 1. Act of bearing or supporting. 2. Doubt, uncertainty. 3. Probability, conjecture. احتمال کرنا iḥtimāl karnā, To doubt, to impute. احتمال هونا iḥtimāl honā, To be probable.
subs: To suppose, presume, &c., to doubt; to attribute or impute (to)
To be allowable or admissible; to be probable, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian احتمال (ihtimāl), from Arabic اِحْتِمَال (iḥtimāl).

ety: acc. of ěḥtěmāl
def: adv. Hypothetically; presumably; probably, most likely, &c.
adj. Hypothetical, problematical, conjectural; presumable; probable, likely; doubtful, questionable, uncertain; suspicious.
more: adj. 1. Presumable; presumptive; likely; probable. 2. Hypothetical ( farzī ); problematical; conjectural. 3. Doubtful; dubious; ambiguous; questionable; suspicious. 4. Contingent; precarious; uncertain.

ety: inf. n. viii of حرق
def: s.m. Burning conflagration; strong desire; vehemence; ardour.
more: (inf. viii. of حرق) s. Burning, conflagration.

ety: inf. n. viii of حرم
def: s.m. Holding in veneration, reverencing, respecting, honouring; paying attention (to); reverence, veneration, respect; deference, attention.
more: (inf. viii. of حرم) s. m. Act of honouring, veneration, reverence, attention, treating with respect.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِحْتِرَام (ihtirām), from Arabic اِحْتِرَام (iḥtirām).

ety: inf. n. viii of حرز
def: s.m. Guarding (against), being cautious (of); abstaining (from); abstinence; regimen; forbearance; controlling the passions and appetites
more: (inf. viii. of حرز) s. m. Abstinence, regimen, abstaining from improper actions of any kind, forbearance, controuling the passions. احتراز کرنا iḥtirāz karnā, To abstain (from), to keep oneʼs self with care (from), to guard oneʼs self (from), to avoid.
subs: (-se), To guard (against), be on (one's) guard (against), protect oneself (from); to refrain (from); to avoid, shun; to abstain (from).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِحْتِرَاز (iḥtirāz).

ety: inf. n. viii of حسب 'To compute'
def: s.m. Making up accounts, reckoning, estimating; — superintendence of police; superintendence of weights and measures in the markets of a town.
more: (inf. viii. of حسب Heb. and Ch. He thought, computed) s. m. 1. Making up accounts, estimating. 2. Superintendence of police: prohibition.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِحْتِسَاب (iḥtisāb).

def: s.m. Superintendent or clerk of the markets, whose business it is to inspect and regulate the weights, measures, qualities, and prices of grain and other market commodities: — s.f. The office of a superintendent or clerk of the markets.

ety: inf. n. viii of حشم
def: s.m. Having many followers or dependants, &c.; pomp, retinue, magnificence.
more: (inf. viii. of حشم) s. m. Pomp, parade, retinue, magnificence.

ety: inf. n. viii of هدي 'to guide aright'
def: s.f. Being guided aright, being directed in the right path (spiritually).

ety: inf. n. viii of هم 'to keep (one) in anxiety'
def: s.m. Solicitude, care, anxiety, diligence; inspection, supervision, management, superintendence, charge
more: (inf. viii. of همّ) s. f. m. Solicitude, care, inspection, anxiety, diligence.
subs: (-meṅ), To be very attentive or careful (in), to show solicitude (in the matter of), to attend carefully (to), look well (after); to overlook, inspect, supervise, superintend, manage, control, carry on (a business, &c.)
(-kā), To put (a thing) in the charge (of), to entrust (to).

def: s.f. = ěhtimām; — s.m. A responsible person, curator, manager, agent.

ety: inf. n. viii of حوج
def: s.f. Necessity, want, need; occasion, exigence; urgency
more: (inf. viii. of حوج) s. f. Necessity, want, occasion, need, exigence.
wety: From Arabic اِحْتِيَاج (iḥtiyāj).

def: s.f. pl. (of ěḥtiyāj), Necessities, wants, requisite things, necessaries.
more: s. plur. (of احتیاج) Requisite things, wants, necessaries.

ety: inf. n. viii of حوط
def: s.f. Caution, care, scrupulousness, attention, heed; vigilance; precaution; circumspection
more: (inf. viii. of حوط) s. f. Caution, circumspection, foresight, scrupulousness, care.
subs: ěḥtiyāt̤ karnā (-meṅ), To exercise caution, care, or circumspection (in), to be careful (in); to mind, attend, heed; to guard, take care (of); to take precautions
adv. Cautiously, with circumspection, warily; carefully, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِحْتِیَاط (ihtiyāt), borrowed from Arabic اِحْتِيَاط (iḥtiyāṭ), from اِحْتَاطَ (iḥtāṭa).

ety: acc. of ěḥtiyāt̤
def: adv. Cautiously, circumspectly, &c. = ěḥtiyāt̤-se, q.v. s.m. The letter or sound e
more: adv. Carefully; cautiously; with care or circumspection; warily; with caution.
subs: adj. (in Gram.), Ending with the letter e (a word).

ety: inf. n. iv of حی 'to live'
def: s.m. The act of making alive, giving life, vivification, quickening; restoring (the dead) to life
subs: Lit. 'Reviving the dead'; (Fig.) bringing waste land into cultivation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian احیا (ihyā), itself from Arabic إِحْيَاء (ʔiḥyāʔ).

ety: inf. n. iv of حضر
def: s.m. Causing to be present, or to appear ( = ḥāẓir karnā); summoning; bringing; summons.
more: (inf. iv. of حضر) s. m. Act of causing to be present; summoning; a summons.

ety: for A. عجالة, v.n. fr. عجل 'to make haste'
def: s.f. Making haste; haste, speed; anticipation (cf. ʻajalat).

ety: inf. n. iv of عجز 'to lack strength' (for), &c.
def: s.m. Disappointment; wonder, astonishment, amazement, surprise, a miracle
more: (inf. iv. of عجز) s. m. Disappointment; miracle, wonder, astonishment, amazement, surprise. اعجاز مسیحائي iﻌjāz-i-masīḥā-ī, A cure as miraculous as the cures of the Messiah, a miraculous cure.
subs: A cure as miraculous as the cures of the Messiah, a miraculous cure.

def: adj. 1. One only; the only one; the same one; the same; the self-same. 2. Alike; equal; uniform. 3. Firm; constant; faithful; true. Woh bhī apnī bāt kā ek hī hai. ek hī ghar ke , Of the same family; from the same ancestor or stock.
adv. Firstly; in the first instance; on the one hand. ek tol , adj. Of the same weight; equal.

ety: see ik
def: pron. One, single, sole, alone, only, a, an; the same, identical; only one; a certain one; single of its kind, unique, singular, preëminent, excellent; — adv. Or so, about (as last member of comp. e.g. pāṅć-ek)
more: adj. S. एकः Pr. ekham, eam ; P. yak . 1. One; a; an; only; only one; a certain one; each; each one; another. 1. Ek anḍā woh bhī gandā . Prov. Only one egg, and that an addled egg. (Said of a bad and only child). 2. Chalo, sakhī, tahāṅ chaleṅ, tahāṅ mileṅ Brij-Rāj, Go-ras bechat Hari mileṅ, ek panth do kāj . Rās Līlā. Come, sister, let us go where we the Lord of Birj shall meet, We'll sell our milk and Harī meet, one stroke. two objects hit. 3. Ek se ek, do se gyārah . Prov. One is but one, from two eleven count on. (One is one, but one next to one is eleven, and it requires two factors to make a product.) 4. Kamalan ko rab ek hai, rab ko kamal anek, Tum ko ham se bahut haiṅ, ham ko tum sā ek. Dohā . The many lotus flowers one sun alone have found, One sun doth many lotus flowers own, For you how many a one like me abound, For me none such as thee, the only One. 5. Ek na shud, do shud . P…

adj. One; only. ايگ آد ek ād, A half, a part, some, some few. ايک بار ek bār or ايک باري ek bār-ī or ايک بارگي ek bārgī, Once, all at once. ايک دست ek dast, Altogether, entire. ايک دل ek dil, Unanimous, of one mind. ايک سان ek sāṅ, adj. Alike, similar, identical. ايک سر ek sar, All at once, all together. ايک سي دن نرهني ek se din na rahne, Is used to denote a change of condition, most commonly from good to evil, but sometimes the reverse. ايک کي دس سناني ek kī das sunānī, To reply ten (words) for one (spoken of a person who replies with many words, especially in giving abuse). ايک قلم ek ḳalam, or ايک لخت ek laḵẖt, All together, entirely, at once. ايک لوتا ek-lautā, adj. Only, single (child without brother or sister). ايک نه ايک ek na ek, One or other.
subs: adj. & s.m. Having one and the same meaning, synonymous; — a synonym ( = ekārth, q.v.)
adj. Of one and the same measure, fashion, &c.; equal, uniform; — ek-andāz-se, adv. After the same pattern or fashion, uniformly, equally
ek-ādh (S. eka+ādi), adj. One or more, one or so, some, some few, few
adj. Having the same form, alike, uniform (see ek-rūp)
adj. One-eyed ( = ekāksh, q.v.); — ek-āṅkh dekhnā, To look with the same eye
adv. In a moment ( = ek pal-meṅ); easily, readily
ek-ek-kar or karke, adv. One by one, separately, singly; severally, each, every, apiece
adv. Two for one, twice as much, double; cent. per cent.
s.f. One and the same thing, all one; one unvarying price
s.f. A distribution of any tax or cess levied on all lands at an equal or uniform rate
ek-biriyān, ek ber, adv. Once, once upon a time; at one time, at once, at the same time, all at once
adv. At once, all at once, suddenly
(S. eka-varna), adj. Of one colour, plain; of one caste, of one race; identical
(S. eka+varṇī), s.f. Beating time, marking musical measure by clapping the hands; an instrument for beating time; a castanet
adv. On or to one side, aside, apart; alongside
adj. One kind, &c. = ek-rang, ek-rūp, q.q.v.
adv. At the same rate or price
s.m. One who eats but one meal a day; a worshipper of one God; — ek-bhakti, adj. & s.f. Of one faith; believing in one and the same deity; — unity or identity of religious belief
adj. Not one, not a single one, none; neither
adv. = ek-bār, q.v. — ek-patnī, s.f. A wife devoted to her husband; a faithful wife
adj. Of the depth of a man's stature
(or -pe) ek, adv. One over or above the other; one after the other; in succession ( = upar-tale)
adj. Of the same womb, uterine, of the same mother, own (brothers or sisters)
s.m. Harmony, unison, concord, the accurate adjustment of song, dance, and instrumental music
s.m. Harmony, &c. ( = ek-tāl, q.v.); — adj. Having the mind fixed on one subject only, closely attentive
adj. Having but one body, not subject to transmigration
adv. In the first instance, firstly; on the one hand
adj. Of the same weight, equal
ek-jā, ek-jagah, adv. In one place, together ( = ikhaṭṭā, q.v.); — ek-jā karnā, v.t. To collect, &c. = ikhaṭṭā karnā, q.v.
s.m. Looking steadily at a thing without blinking; fixed look, stare, gaze; — adv. Steadily, fixedly; — ek-ṭak dekhnā, v.t. To look steadily or fixedly at, to scan carefully, eye, gaze at
ek-ẕāt, adj. Of the same caste, race, family, or sort; co-religionist; of the same parents
ek-jigar, adj. Having one soul, or one heart; true, sincere ( = ek-dil, q.v.)
adj. Collected as to thought, thinking of one thing only; intent upon, absorbed in; of one mind, unanimous, concurring, agreeing; unwavering, determined, resolute; (cf, ek-dil and ek-mat)
lit. 'Two halves of a chick-pea'; two equal shares; brothers of the whole blood
(S. eka-kshatra), s.m. Supreme monarch, emperor; — (hence) Rājā Rāmćandra
adv. Altogether, entirely ( = ek laḵẖt, ek-musht)
adv. Once; once upon a time
adj. Having or feeling the same joys and sorrows, sympathizing, sympathetic
adj. Of one heart or soul, of one mind, unanimous; single-minded, true, sincere, faithful
ek-dam-meṅ, adv. In one breath, in a moment, at once, all at once
adj. & s.m. Belonging to the same place or country; — fellow-countryman
adj. & adv. In one piece, without joining
ek t̤aur-ke, adj. Of one make or fashion, of the same sort ( = ek-rūp, ek-sā); uniform
adv. After the same fashion or manner; symmetrically
s.m. One only flavour; one sole pleasure; — adj. Pleased with one object only, unchanged, unchangeable, constant
adv. In one payment; at once
adj. Of one colour; of one sort or kind, similar, alike; constant, true, sincere ( = ek-dil); — ek-rangā, s.m. A red cloth commonly worn by native women, (syn. qand)
ek-rūpī, adj. Having the same shape, form or appearance, alike, similar, resembling ( = ek ḍaul)
adj. Of the same speech; of the same mind, consentient ( = ek-dil, ek-mat); — adv. With one voice, with one accord, unanimously ( = ek-muṅh, q.v.)

ek-sāṅ, ek-sār, adj. (f. ek-sī), Alike, having the same qualities, of the same sort, similar, corresponding; identical; — ek-se, adv. One and the same, unvarying, unchanging; — ek-se din na rahnā, The days or time not to remain the same, but to change (usually from bad to good)
ek-sang, adv. Together, in a body, simultaneously, all at once, suddenly
adv. All at once; all together
adj. One (better or worse) than another
s.m. One only hope and refuge, epithet of any deity
adj. Of one body; of one blood
adj. On one side, aside, apart ( = ek-bag̠al, q.v.)
adj. Yielding but one crop annually
adv. At a stroke of the pen; at once, bodily, entirely, altogether ( = ek laḵẖt)
s.m. One time, one and the same time; — adv. At one time; once
adv. One after the other, one by one ( = ek-ek-kar, q.v.)
adv. One behind, or after, the other, in succession, in a line, row, train, &c.
To give back ten (words) for one, to pay back with interest, to give (one) a volley of abuse
To divide into two equal parts, to bisect; to cut in two
(-ko), lit. 'To drive with one and the same stick'; to rule all men with the same rod, to treat all alike
adv. Altogether, at once, entirely, in the mass, bodily
adj. & s.m. Having or consisting of one string; — a string of pearls, gold ornaments, &c. worn on the neck; necklace of a single string of pearls, &c.
ek-lauṭā, adj. Only, single (child. without brother or sister = ik-lautā, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Having a single mātra; (in Gram.), a short vowel
adj. Of the same parents; — ek mā-bāp-ke honā, or ho-jānā, To be or live as brothers, to live in peace and harmony; to sing in unison or harmony
adj. Of one way of thinking, of one mind, of one opinion, consentient, agreeing, unanimous
s.f. A handful; — adv. Altogether, at once, in the lump or mass; in one payment
adj. & s.m. Having the same meaning, synonymous; — synonym ( = ek-arthī)

adj. & adv. Agreeing, united, unanimous, &c.; — adv. With one voice, with one consent, unanimously ( = ek zabān); — ek muṅh honā, or ho-jānā, To be unanimous, to concur, be of the same opinion; — to sing in concert, harmony, or chorus
adj. Of the same value or price
adj. Having the same name, cognominal
Not one, no one, none; neither
adj. Of one and the same fashion, kind, sort; regular, uniform, equal ( = ek andāz, q.v.)
adj. One alone, sole one, a single; just the same, all one, alike, identical; unique, unparalleled; — ek-hī honā (meṅ or kā), To stand alone (in respect of), be unparalleled or unique.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi एक (eka), from Apabhramsa एक्क (ekka), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓 (ekka), from Sanskrit एक (éka). Doublet of یَک (yak).

ety: S. एक+कः
def: s.m. Unity, union, unanimity; combination, league, compact, conspiracy; a unit; an ace (at cards = ikkā)
more: n. m. 1. Unanimity; union; concurrence; accord; concord. 2. Alliance; league; compact; combination; conspiracy. Saboṅ ne ekā kar līyā . 3. Chem . Affinity; adhesion; attraction.
subs: v.n. To act in unison or concert, to act together, act simultaneously; to combine, unite, conspire; ekā-ek, ekā-ekī, adv. All at once, simultaneously; suddenly.

def: adj. Some; some few; few; hardly, or scarcely any. Wahāṅ to ek-ād ādmī khaṛā hī rahtā hai. ek-arthī, ek-mānī-kā , n. m. A synonym. ek-andāz, ek parmān, ek wazٴa , adj. Equal; uniform; of the same fashion. ek āṅkh dekhnā , v. n. To look with the same eye. ek andāz se , adv. Equally; on the same scale. ek aur , adj. Another; one more. ek ek, ek ek kar-ke, ek ke bād ek , adv. One by one; one after the other; consecutively; seriatim ; individually; each; every; apiece; severally; singly. 1. Ek ek kauṛī ko tarastā hai . 2. Chār chor, chaurāsī banye, ek ek kar-ke lūṭā . Prov. Four thieves plundered eighty-four banyās (a timid caste) one after the other. 3. Mastī meṅ ek ek pe girtā hai ek ek . Bedil. ek ek ke do do , Twice as much; double; hundred per cent. ek bāt , n. f. lit . One word. 1. The same thing; no difference; no matter. Sab ek bāt hai . It is all one. 2. One price; no variation or bargaining. Hamāre hāṅ ek bāt hai . We have but one price. ek bār ; Rus. ek bar, ik biryāṅ , adv. Once; once upon a time.
adv. Once, once upon a time; at one time, at once.
adj. Eleven ( = iggyārah).
adj. Eleventh.
s.f. The eleventh day of a lunar fortnight.
s. f. The eleventh day of the lunar fortnight.

ety: eka+adhipati
def: s.m. Sole and absolute monarch.
s.m. Sole and absolute sovereignty.
All at once, suddenly.
adv. All at once, suddenly.
see s.v. ekā
subs: cf. also P. yak-ā-yak. s.m. Unity
union, unanimity; combination, league, compact, conspiracy; a unit; an ace (at cards = ikkā)
v.n. To act in unison or concert, to act together, act simultaneously; to combine, unite, conspire; ekā-ek, ekā-ekī, adv. All at once, simultaneously; suddenly.

ety: eka+agra
def: adj. Having one point, fixing the attention on only one point or object, closely attentive; intent; undisturbed, unperplexed; — s.m. One whose mind is fixed on one object
subs: adj. & s.m. Having an attentive mind, attentive, intent upon, &c. = ekāgra.

def: s.f. Intentness in the pursuit of one object, close and undisturbed attention.
adj. (f. -ī), Alone, solitary; single; of one twist, of one piece, &c.; thin ( = ikahrā, q.v.).
adj. One only, one and the same, &c. ( = ek-hī, q.v.); — s.f. Units = ekāʼī, q.v.

ety: from ekā
def: s.f. Loneliness, solitariness, solitude; unity, unanimity; units (in Numeration).

ety: eka+eka
def: adv. Everyone, each successively, one by one, &c. ( = ek ek kar, q.v.).
adv. One by one, singly, &c. ( = ek ek karke).
adj. Single; solitary, alone; the same (cf. ekal).
adj. Alone, single, solitary, sole.
adj. Alone, single, solitary, sole.

ety: eka + aksha
def: adj. One-eyed, blind of one eye; having one axle; — s.m. A crow.
adj. Alone, solitary ( = akelā); — s.m. A solitary person.
adj. One; — s.f. The first day of the Hindū fortnight
more: n. f. The first day of the Hindu fortnight. ekam-ek, ekam-kār , adj. 1. Mixed up; heterogenous; disordered; disarranged; unclassified; in a state of chaos. 2. Rendered unclean or impure by touch. ek-mol , adj. Of the same value or price. ek-muṅh, ek-zabān , adj. Agreeing; united; unanimous; of one speech; of the same mind. ek-muṅh, ek-zabān , adv. Unanimously; with one consent; with one voice; with one accord; una voce ; by common consent; in chorus; as one man; to a man; nem con, or nemine contradicente; nemine dissentiente ; without a dissentient voice; one and all. ek muṅh ho'nā , v. n. 1. To agree; coincide in opinion ( ek rāë honā ); to be unanimous. Sab ek-muṅh ho gaë . 2. To be of the same mind; be at one with; enter into one's views; sing in chorus; chime in. ek-musht, ek-raqam , adv. In one payment.
subs: ekam-ek, Made one of, mixed, jumbled, confused, disordered; caused to be apart or alone, rendered unclean or impure (by touch).

def: adj. Ninety-one: ( = ikānwe, q.v.).

ety: eka+anta
def: s.m. A lonely, retired, or secret place; seclusion, retirement, privacy; an extreme; — adj. Secluded, sequestered, retired, lonely, solitary, secret, private; single, unaccompanied; extreme, excessive; — adv. Apart, aside, alone; privately, secretly; — extremely, excessively
more: adj. S. अन्त limit. Secluded; sequestered; retired; private; lonely; solus ; solitary; single; unaccompanied.

ek-lauṭā, adj. Only, single (child. without brother or sister = ik-lautā, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Having a single mātra; (in Gram.), a short vowel
adj. Of the same parents; — ek mā-bāp-ke honā, or ho-jānā, To be or live as brothers, to live in peace and harmony; to sing in unison or harmony
adj. Of one way of thinking, of one mind, of one opinion, consentient, agreeing, unanimous
s.f. A handful; — adv. Altogether, at once, in the lump or mass; in one payment
adj. & s.m. Having the same meaning, synonymous; — synonym ( = ek-arthī)

adj. & adv. Agreeing, united, unanimous, &c.; — adv. With one voice, with one consent, unanimously ( = ek zabān); — ek muṅh honā, or ho-jānā, To be unanimous, to concur, be of the same opinion; — to sing in concert, harmony, or chorus
adj. Of the same value or price
adj. Having the same name, cognominal
Not one, no one, none; neither
adj. Of one and the same fashion, kind, sort; regular, uniform, equal ( = ek andāz, q.v.)
adj. One alone, sole one, a single; just the same, all one, alike, identical; unique, unparalleled; — ek-hī honā (meṅ or kā), To stand alone (in respect of), be unparalleled or unique.

def: adj. Cognominal; having the same name. ek na, ek bhī nahīṅ , No one; none; not a single one; neither. ek na ek , Conj. Either; one or the other.

ety: S. eka+uttara+kah
def: adj. & s.m. One over a hundred, one hundred and one; — one per cent.
more: adj. 1. A hundred and one. Us ne baṭairī meṅ ikotar dīye . 2. One per cent. Ekotre kā byāj .

ety: S. eka+uttara+kah
def: adj. & s.m. One over a hundred, one hundred and one; — one per cent.
s.m. The figure one (٩).
n. m. The figure one (?). ek-sāth, ek-saṅg , adv. Together; together with; hand in hand; at the same time.
W., n. m. A red cloth commonly worn by native women. ek rupayā saikṛā , One rupee per cent per month.

adj. Alike; similar; corresponding; matching. ek se ek , E.;— — — chaṛhtā, yā baṛhtā , W., adj. lit . One more than the other. Exceeding; excelling; surpassing. Dunyā meṅ ek se ek chaṛhtā hai. ek-se din na rahnā , v. n. Not to remain constant. Applied to a change of condition, most commonly from bad to good, but sometimes the reverse. Sadā ek-se din nahin rahte . Prov. Saying. ek shakhs , n. A certain individual or person. ek-tarfah , adj. One-sided; partial; ex parte ; biassed; prejudiced. ek-tarfah ḍigrī , n. One-sided decree. ek-faslī , adj. Yielding but one crop annually. ek ke pīchhe ek , adv. One after the other; one following the other; successively; continually; in a train. ek kī das sunānā , v. a. lit . To give back ten (words) for one. To pour down a shower of abuse; to pay back with interest. ek ke do karnā , v. a. To bisect; to divide into two equal parts; cut into two. ek-qalam ; P. yak-qalam , adv. lit . One pen. At a stroke; at once; entirely; altogether ( bilkul ). ek lāṭhī hāṅknā , v. n. lit . To drive with one stick. To rule all men with one rod; to use or treat indiscriminately. Gadhe, ghoṛe ko ek lāṭhī hāṅkte ho! Prov. What! use the same stick on a horse and a donkey! ek mā bāp ke , adj. Of the same parents; the whole blood. ek mā bāp ke honā , v. n. 1. To live as brethren; live in peace and good will. 2. To sing in harmony; sing in chorus; chime with. Ek mā bāp ke ho jāo . Players. ek muāmlah, ghar kī bāt , A domestic affair; a transaction between friends.
adj. Forty-one: ( = iktālīs, q.v.). adj. Forty-one.

(one leg of a quadruped), māshā , (Broker's idiom); chavannī , n. f. A four-anna piece. ek-jā, ek-jagah, ikhaṭṭā, ek-thān , adv. In one place; collected together; in a mass. ek-jā karnā , v. a. See ikhaṭṭā karnā. ek-jāt, ek-zāt ; P. ham-qaum , adj. Of the same caste, family, race, kind, or sort; a co-religionist; of the same parents. ek-jāt, ek-mat , Prov. One caste, one mind. A term used in agreements, contracts, and the like, by which the subscribers bind themselves jointly and severally to fulfil the terms specified. ek-jān, ek-jigar, ek-dil , n. m. One soul, or heart; true or bosom friends. ek-chit , adj. Of one mind. ek chane kī do dāl, lit . One pea, two halves. 1. Two equal shares. W. 2. E. Brothers of the whole blood. ek dam meṅ, ek lakht , adv. In one breath; in an instant; in one moment; at once; all at once; in a trice. ek divāl rupayā , (Broker's idiom). One thousand Rupees. ek dūs're kī madad , Co-operation with one another; mutual help. ek-de'sī; P. ham watan , adj. Belonging to the same place or country; a fellow-countryman. ek-ḍaul, bhāṅt, raṅg, rūp, sūrat, yā taur ke , adj. Of the same sort; of one make ( eksāṅ ); uniform. ek-ḍhaṅg se , adv. Symmetrically; in the same order. ek-raṅg, yak-raṅg , Opp. of du-raṅgā , adj. 1. Of one colour; similar; equal; same. Nāj kā bhāo sab jagah ik-raṅg hai . 2. Sincere; honest; true ( sachchā ).
adj. Thirty-one ( = iktīs, q.v.).
more: adj. Thirty one: see اکتيس iktīs.

def: s.f. Cardamoms. (See ilāćī.)

ety: inf. n. iv of علم 'to know'
def: s.m. Proclaiming, indicating, announcing; teaching; distinguishing oneself (in battle); distinction; notification, notice, citation, warrant.
more: (inf. iv. of علم) s. m. Proclaiming, indicating, announcing; a notification, a warrant, citation, notice.

ety: inf. n. iv of علن 'to become public'
def: s.m. Divulging, manifesting, publishing; publication; declaration; proclamation.
more: (inf. iv. of علن) s. m. Divulging, manifesting, publishing.

def: n. f. Embassy; the office or dignity of an ambassador.
s.m. Ambassador, envoy, delegate, agent
more: n. m. T. yalchī , An envoy; delegate; ambassador.

s. m. An ambassadour, envoy, agent, attorney. ايلچي پنا elchī-panā, s. m. Ambassadourship, agency. ايلچي گري elchī-garī, s. f. Office of ambassadour, embassage, embassy. ايلچي گري کرنا elchī-garī karnā, To act the ambassadour, &c., to act for another.
subs: To discharge the functions of an ambassador or agent; to act in another's stead.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ایلْچِی (ēlčī), from Turkic, ultimately from Proto-Turkic *ēlči, from *ēl (“realm, country”) + *-či (profession suffix). Compare Azerbaijani elçi, Turkish elçi, Uzbek elchi and Gujarati એલચી (elcī).

def: s.f. = H ايلچيپنا elćī-panā, s.m. The office or dignity of an ambassador, &c.; ambassadorship, embassy, agency.
s.m. = P ايلچيگري elćī-garī, s.f. The office or dignity of an ambassador, &c.; ambassadorship, embassy, agency.
= الغار ilg̠ār, q.v. (The ي of this word is merely scriptio plena).
(corr. of yā 'alī yā allāh), intj, O ʻAlī! O God! (used by lascars or Indian sailors when pulling ropes, &c.).
s.m. Aloes (syn. muṣabbar).

ety: inf. n. iv of عمل 'to work'
def: s.m. Causing, or teaching, to act or operate; applying.

ety: for A. āmin, by imālah
def: adj. Safe, secure, void of care.
part. adj. Pulled, drawn, &c.
subs: adj. Stretched and strained, pulled eross-wise, &c.; squint-eyed
s.f. Stretching and straining, tugging and pulling; scuffle, tussle, scrimmage, struggle; difficulty, entanglement, toils.

ety: v.n. of next, q.v.
def: s.f. Pulling, drawing, dragging, &c.; struggle, difficulty, toils, &c. ( = eṅćā-tānī, q.v.).

adj. Squint-eyed. Sau par phullā, hazār par kānā, Savā lākh par eṅchā-tānā . Prov. One man 'gainst a hundred a cataract-eye, For a thousand a man with one eye is a tie, With ten myriads and one-fourth the squint-eyed may vie.

n. f. 1. Stretching and straining; tussle; set to; battle. 1. Eṅchan chhoṛ, ghasīṭan meṅ paṛe . Prov. From hauling he was hauled himself. (The bee caught in its own honey). 2. Saut jo ābegī, laṛāi hovegī, To eṅchā-tānī hovegī saīyāṅ ko . W. Rus. Song. The co-wife come, a row there 'll be, The husband in a stew you 'll see. 3. Do jurvāṅ pānī chalīṅ eṅchā-tānī huī, Bhaṛve, taine kyoṅ byāhīṅ do janyāṅ? Song. Sautan. Two co-wives gone to water fetch fell foul of one another— "Thou wh—e-son why took'st thou two wives for plagues to one another"? 2. Struggles; straits; difficulties; up-hill work. Āj-kal ham muqadme kī eṅchan meṅ phaṅse huë haiṅ. eṅch-tān-ke , adv. With difficulty; hardly; by hook or by crook. Eṅch tān-ke pūrā ḍālā .

ety: S. अंह्त्रि 'foot'
def: s.f. Heel ( = eṛī); spur; a riding cane or stick
more: s. f. Spurring; spur. ايڙمارنا eṛ mārnā, To spur.
subs: eṛ lagāna, eṛ mārnā (-ko), To strike (a horse) with the heel; to urge (a horse) on with the heel; to apply the spur, to spur.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अंह्रि (aṃhri).

def: n. f. S. अंहि a foot. The spur, etc. for urging on a horse. eṛ karo, ṭahlo, havā khāo, chāl dikhāo, chalte phirte nazar āo , intj. (Slang). Be off; go away; out upon you! eṛ mār'nā, karnā, yā lagānā , v. a. To spur; apply the spur. Tum bhī hoë savār, lagāo eṛ, hāth lo koṛā, Shastar bāṅdh kamar se apnī, pahn shikārī joṛā . Play. Chaubolā .

ety: inf. n. iv of عرب 'to become chaste' (Arabic)
def: s.m. The vowels or diacritical points in Arabic.

ety: inf. n. iv of عرج 'to limp'
def: s.m. Making lame, crippling.
more: (inf. iv. of عرج) s. Making lame.

ety: S. एडकः
def: s.m. A ram; a large ram or he goat trained for fighting; — a kind of fireworks.

r: eraṇḍ, eraṇḍī, Palma christi, Ricinus communis, anḍī.
def: s.m. = H ايرنڐي एरण्ड़ी eraṇḍī, s.f. The castor-oil plant, Palma christi, or Ricinus communis. (See anḍī.)

def: s.f. = S ايرنڐ एरण्ड़ eraṇḍ, s.m. The castor-oil plant, Palma christi, or Ricinus communis. (See anḍī.)

def: m. = H ايراپهيري एराफेरी er-ā-pherī, f. (redupl. of pher, with conjunctive ā), Exchange, interchange; barter; dancing attendance; roaming or wandering to and fro ( = herā-pherī); combination, collusion; (see er-pher).
more: s. m. Exchange, interchange; barter, dancing attendance.

r: er-ā-pherī, erā-pher, pher, ā, herā-pherī, er-pher
def: f. = H ايراپهير एराफेर erā-pher, m. (redupl. of pher, with conjunctive ā), Exchange, interchange; barter; dancing attendance; roaming or wandering to and fro ( = herā-pherī); combination, collusion; (see er-pher).
more: n. f. H. ānā to come, phernā to return. Frequent changing of one thing for another. 1. Exchange; change; substitution. 2. Frequent coming to the house; dancing attendance. Chor chorī se gayā, to kyā herā pherī se bhī gayā? Prov. The thief may give up his calling, but his old haunts will haunt him still. (He delights in reviving the old associations).
s. f. Exchange, interchange; barter, dancing attendance.

ety: inf. n. iv of عرض 'to turn'
def: s.m. Turning aside (from), flying (from), avoiding; aversion, dislike
more: (inf. iv. of عرض) s. m. Aversion, avoiding, dislike, averseness. اعراض کرنا iﻌrāẓ karnā, To avoid, turn from, decline.
subs: (-se), To turn (from), to avoid, shun; to decline; to be averse (from).

ety: S. एड+गज
def: s.m. The medicinal plant Cassia tora or Alata (used for the cure of ring-worm).

ety: see ṭeṛhā
def: adj. & s.m. (f. eṛhī-ṭeṛhī), Crooked, awry; irregular; cross-grained; inconsiderate, &c. ( = atpatī, q.v.)

ety: eṛ, q.v.+S. इका
def: s.f. Heel
more: s. f. The heel. ايڙي ديکهو eṛī dekho, Look at your heel (look at home): a phrase used to obviate the effect of an evil eye.
subs: lit. 'Look at (your, -apnī) heels'; look at home (a phrase used by Mohammadan women to obviate the effect of evil eye)
eṛiyāṅ ghisnā, To drag the heels in walking; to rub the heels (against the bed in dying); to be in straitened circumstances, to be in distress, or in agony.
wety: From ایڑ (eṛ), from Sanskrit अंह्रि (aṃhri).

def: n. f. The heel. Choṭī kā pasīnā eṛī ko ātā hai, jab ṭakā paidā hotā hai . When the perspiration on the forehead flows down to the heel, then only I can earn a penny. eṛī choṭī pe qurbān karnā, wārnā, yā nisār kar'nā , v. a. Mah. Wom. To turn any dead or living thing round from heel to head as an offering or sacrifice for one's welfare. Jo mere bachche ko hūṅse, use apnī eṛī choṭī par qurbān karūṅ . Mah. Wom. Whoever casts an evil eye upon my child, from head to foot I'll make a sacrifice of him. eṛī dekh, lit . Look at your heels. Look at home. A phrase used by Mah. Wom. to avert the effects of an evil eye. Kahā Raṅgīṅ ne, "hai dhuāṅ tezī" Gore pāoṅ meṅ nīlī beṛī dekh, Pahle kuchh buṛ-buṛā-ke hoṅṭoṅ meṅ, Kahā maiṅ ne ki "apnī eṛī dekh" . Wom. Rangin. "How beautiful," Rangīṅ did say to me, "Those ankles fair, the chain of blue thread grips!" I muttered some abuse between my lips, Then said to him "Do you your own heels see." eṛī se choṭī tak , adv. From head to heel. See ād se ant tak (1). eṛiyāṅ ragaṛ'nā, ghisnā, yā ragaṛ-ke marnā , 1. To drag along the heels in walking; rub the heels against the bed in dying; to die a lingering or hard death. Chārpāī pe kaun paṛ-ke mare? Kaun yūṅ eṛiyāṅ ragaṛ-ke mare? Wom. Shauq. Who would bed-ridden be before he die, A death endure of long-drawn agony? 2. To be in straitened circumstances; to die of want ( pāoṅ pīṭnā ). Sakhī, karīm paṛe eṛiyāṅ ragaṛte haiṅ, Bakhīl motyoṅ ko mūsaloṅ se chhaṛte haiṅ . Nazīr. The gen'rous good their heels now rub against the bed, The miser pearls with pestle grinds away instead. (The undeserving roll in wealth).
more: interj. O! A vocative particle for females.

def: s.m. Reproving; reproof, reproach, censure, reprimand, rebuke; anger, displeasure; — ʻětāb karnā (-par), To pronounce censure (on), to censure, rebuke, &c.

W. adj. pro. This; what do you call it? ( kyā nām ).?

ety: inf. n. viii of عبر 'to try or examine'
def: s.m. Confidence, trust, reliance, faith, belief; respect, esteem, repute; credit, authority, credibility; weight, importance; regard, respect, view, consideration, reference
more: (inf. viii. of عبر Heb. and Ch. He passed over) s. m. 1. Confidence, trust, faith, belief, credit, reliance, respect, esteem. 2. Taking as an example, reasoning by comparison. اعتبار رکهنا iﻌtibār rakhnā, To give credit to, to believe, to trust.
subs: (-kā), ěʻtibār karnā (-kā), To give credit (to), to believe, trust, rely (upon), confide (in), depend (upon); to respect, hold in esteem; — to feel assured, confident, &c.
adj. Beyond belief or credit, incredible, untrustworthy
v.n. To lose credit or repute.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِبَار (i'tibār), borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِبَار (iʕtibār), from اِعْتَبَرَ (iʕtabara).

def: adj. Of credit, creditable, trustworthy, worthy of confidence, credible; reputable; confidential, trusty.
more: adj. Of credit, creditable, trust-worthy; a confidant.

more: (inf. viii. of عدل) s. m. Evenness, equilibrium, moderation, temperateness, temperature, mean, moderateness, rectitude.
subs: To follow the happy mean; to observe moderation, &c.
(mizāj-kā), To be in a good or sound state of health.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِدَال (i'tidāl), borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِدَال (iʕtidāl), from اِعْتَدَلَ (iʕtadala).

subs: s.m. Small matter; small quantity; trifle
adv. By this time; all this time
adv. In the meantime, meanwhile; at this moment; upon this.

subs: (-kā), To place confidence or faith (in), believe (in); rely (on), trust (in), depend (upon)
ěʻtiqād lānā (-kā), To bring forward or evince faith (in), have faith (in), believe, put trust (in); to become a convert (to), be converted (to).

subs: To sit secluded in a temple or mosque, abstaining from worldly affairs, &c., and engaging in devotional exercises; to retire from the world ( = dunyā tyāgnā).
