def: s.m. Reliance, dependence, trust, confidence, faith
more: n. m. عمد leaning upon. Ped. for H. partīt , q. v. Reliance; dependence ( bharosah ). etemād rakh'nā,—kar'nā , v. n. To rely, believe; trust; confide. etemād karā'nā , v. a. To cause to be believed.
(inf. viii. of عمد) s. m. Reliance, trust, faith, confidence, dependence. اعتماد رکهنا یا کرنا iﻌtimād-rakhnā or -karnā, To rely, to depend (on).
subs: (-par), ěʻtimād karnā (-par), To place reliance (on), rely (on), depend (on), to confide (in), trust, believe; to rest or incline (on)
v.t. To cause to be trusted or believed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِمَاد (i'timād), from Arabic اِعْتِمَاد (iʕtimād).

def: adj. & s.m. To be trusted or relied on or believed, reliable, trustworthy; — one in whom confidence is placed, a trustworthy person.

ety: inf. n. viii of عنی 'to disquiet'
def: s.f. Disquietude, anxiety, care; attention; study, labour, taking pains.
more: (inf. viii. of عني Heb. Was concerned or afflicted) s. f. Care, attention, study, labour.

ety: inf. n. viii of عرف 'to know'
def: s.m. Acknowledgment, avowal, confession
more: (inf. viii. of عرف) s. m. Confession, acknowledgment, avowal. اعتراف کرنا iﻌtirāf karnā, To confess, acknowledge, allow.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِرَاف (iʕtirāf).

ety: inf. n. viii of عرض 'to place (something) before one'
def: s.f. Opposition; refusing assent, objecting; objection; criticism, animadversion, strictures, fault-finding; displeasure
more: (inf. viii. of عرض) s. f. Opposition, refusing assent, objecting, objection, discussion, criticism, animadversion. اعتراض کرنا iﻌtirāẓ karnā, To object, to except.
subs: (-par), To object (to), animadvert (on), criticize; carp (at), find fault (with), to call in question, dispute, take exception (to), impugn; to protest against.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِعْتِرَاض (i'tirāz), borrowed from Arabic اِعْتِرَاض (iʕtirāḍ), from اِعْتَرَضَ (iʕtaraḍa).

def: s.f. = اعتراض ěʻtirāẓ, q.v.; — adj. & s.m. Objecting, &c.; — one who objects.

ety: inf. n. viii of عصم 'to preserve or keep'
def: s.m. Preserving oneself from sin, abstaining from what is sinful or unlawful.
more: (inf. viii. of عصمت) s. m. Abstaining from sin or what is unlawful.

ety: inf. n. viii of عزل 'to put or set apart'
def: s.m. Secession, dissent; withdrawing from office; abdication.
more: (inf. viii. of عزل) s. m. Secession, abdication.

ety: inf. n. viii of عذر 'to excuse'
def: s.m. Excuse, apology
more: (inf. viii. of عذر) s. m. Excuse, apology.

def: adj. & s.m. Apologizing; — one who apologizes.

ety: inf. n. viii of عزّ 'to be strong,' &c.
def: s.m. Prevalence; excellence.
adj. So much, &c. = itnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), As much as this, this much; so much, so many
more: adj. So much, &c. see اتنا itnā.
subs: s.m. Small matter; small quantity; trifle
adv. By this time; all this time
adv. In the meantime, meanwhile; at this moment; upon this.

def: adj. So much, &c. = ittā, itnā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī) & adv. This much or many, &c. ( = اتنا itnā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), As much as this, this much; so much, so many
more: adj. So much, so, thus much.
subs: s.m. Small matter; small quantity; trifle
adv. By this time; all this time
adv. In the meantime, meanwhile; at this moment; upon this.

def: s.m. Sunday = itwār, q.v. s.m. Sunday.
more: s. m. Sunday: see اتوار itwār.

def: s.m. A debtor, who avails himself of Sunday to make his appearance abroad without fear of arrest.
adv. As, like; even so, verily, indeed; only, still, just, also.
adv. So, thus, also; yes.

ety: S. अवि+कट
def: s.m. Flock of goats or sheep.

ety: pl. of عيل ʻaiyil, fr. عول, or inf. n. of عول 'to nourish or sustain a family'
def: s.m. Family, children; household; domestics
more: n. m. pl. of عيل A family (اولاد). ayāl-dār , n. m. A man with a family or large family. ayāl-dārī , n. f. Family or worldly affairs.
(plur. of عيل aﻌiyil, from عول or عيل To support a family, Heb. To give suck, also A suckling) s. m. Family, children, domesticks. عيالدار iﻌyāl-dār, Having a family.
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a family, or a large family; — a man with a (large) family
s.f. Family or household affairs; worldly affairs.

ety: inf. n. of عاين 'to see with the eye, to see face to face'
def: adj. Clear, evident, visible, manifest, open, public, conspicuous (syn. āshkār)
more: (from عين Heb. The eye) adj. Clear, manifest, publick, conspicuous, visible, apparent, evident.
subs: v.t. To make clear or manifest, to lay bare or open; to elucidate, &c.

ety: inf. n. iv of عز 'to become honourable,' &c.
def: s.m. Honouring, magnifying; honour, respect, esteem, attention ( = ʻizzat).
more: (inf. iv. of عزت) s. m. Honouring, magnifying, respect, attention, honour, esteem.

ety: Zend yazata, part. fr. rt. yaz = S. यजत, rt. यज्
def: s.m. God.
wety: From Classical Persian ایزد (ēzad, “God”).

def: adj. Divine; bestowed for the love of God.
wety: From Persian ایزدی, from Persian noun ایزد, from Avestan yazata "worthy of reverence". Compare Sanskrit यजत (yajata).

def: conj. And, and so, and thereupon, and consequently, therefore, then, &c. (e.g. fa-qat, q.v.).
more: 20th letter of the Arabic, and 23rd of the Persian alphabet. In Sanskrit and Hindi ph is substituted for it. In abjad it stands for 80.

def: (for A. فعلة, pl. of fāʻil), s.m. pl. Workers (in clay, &c.); agents.
more: s. m. 1. Deed, action. 2. (plur. of فاعل) Agents. ¶ fiﻌla, See فعل fiﻌl.

def: adv. So be it; so much the better.
(contract. of فجاٴبالخصلة الحسنة fajāʼa bi-l-ḵẖaṣlati-l-ḥasanati ) interj. Very well! then it is well, excellent! bravo!

ety: fa+bi+hā, qq.v.
def: intj. Then be it so; well and good; very well; excellent! bravo!
s. Name of a village in Ḵẖaibar.
s.m. = pād-zahr, q.v. s.m. Antidote, Bezoar stone.

n. m. pl. favāid , فيد gain. 1. Advantage; benefit; interest ( paidā ). 2. Outturn; yield. 3. Value; importance; service; use. 4. Convalescence ( softah , 2). 5. Moral; inference; result; corollary. fāëdah uṭhānā , v. n. See pānā , (1, 2). 1. To reap benefit or advantage (اتَهانا 18). 2. To utilize; turn to account; make the most of. fāëdah-i-zātī . Self-interest. fāëdah-i-zimnī . An incidental advantage. fāëdah k. , v. 1. To benefit; profit; avail; serve; to be beneficial. 2. To do good to; to improve; cure. fāëdah-mand , adj. 1. Advantageous ( achchhā , 21, 25). 2. Conducive, subservient, or favorable to. 3. Efficacious; efficient. fāëdah h. , v. n. To derive benefit, etc.; to avail. be-fāëdah , adj. See birthā , and bekār 3, 4. 1. Unserviceable; of no use; unavailing; unadvisable. 2. Needless. 3. Absurd. be-fāëdah , adv. Unprofitably; in vain; uselessly.
more: (from فيد) s. m. Profit, gain, advantage, utility, benefit, interest. فايده مند fāʼịda-mand, adj. Profitable, with profit, gain, or advantage, advantageous. فاٴيده اٿهـانا fāʼida uṭhānā, To reap benefit. فاٴيده کرنا fāʼida karnā, To benefit, to profit.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic فَائِدَة (fāʔida).

ety: for bag̠pūr; Pehl. bagh-pūr; Zend bagha+pourū?; S. भग+पुरु
def: s.m. The title of the emperor of China.
more: s. m. The emperour of China.

ety: intens. n. fr. فحش; see fāḥish
def: adj. Gross, lewd, obscene; talking indecently, &c. ( = fāḥish).
more: adj. (superl. of فحش) Obscene, talking indecently.

def: s.f. Obscenity, &c. ( = fǒḥsh).
adj. Intelligent. Burbak ke dhan fahīma nar khāë . Prov. Sharp men enjoy the wealth of fools.

ety: v.n. fr. فہم; see fāhm
def: adj. Of great understanding or intellect; very intelligent; learned.

ety: act. part. of فحش 'to be excessive, or immoderate,' &c.
def: adj. Excessive, immoderate, enormous, exorbitant; foul, evil, bad, abominable, nefarious; gross, immodest, impudent, shameless, indecent, lewd, obscene.
more: adj. فحش being shameful. Enormous; nefarious ( bhārī , 19). Fāhish galatī hai .
(part. act. of فحش) adj. Obscene, indecent, impudent, shameful, gross.

ety: for A. فاحشة fāhishat, fem. of fāḥish
def: s.f. A lewd or shameless woman, a harlot; — an enormity; anything abominable.
more: (from فحش) s. f. 1. A harlot. 2. Any thing abominable.

ety: inf. n. of فہم 'to understand,' &c.
def: s.m. Understanding, conception, perception, apprehension, comprehension, intellect, intelligence, sense; — (in Pers., rt. of fahm-īdan, fr. A. fahm), act. part. Understanding, apprehending, perceiving, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. suḵẖan-fahm, 'understanding a speech or discourse')
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَهم, from Arabic فَهْم (fahm).

def: n. f. Sense; understanding; intelligence. zūd-fahm , adj. Having quick perception; sharp-witted; acute ( tez ). zūd-fahmī , n. f. Quickness of perception; quick parts. kam-fahmī , n. f. Defective understanding.
more: part. act. (of فـﮩميدن which from Arab. فـﮩم) in compos. Understanding, perceiving.

def: (dakh.) To explain, to instruct.
n. f. 1. Instructions; directions; injunction. 2. Expostulation; warning; caution. fahmāyash k .; v. a. 1. To enjoin; direct; instruct; warn ( sīkh denā ). 2. To exhort; impress upon.

r: fahmāyish, fahmāʼish, fahmā, fahmāy, fahmānīdan, fahmīdan
ety: abst. s. fr. fahmā, or fahmāy, rt. of fahmānīdan, caus. of fahmīdan
def: s.f. 'Causing or giving to understand,' informing; instructions, directions; admonition, warning, caution
subs: v.t. To inform, instruct, direct; to admonish, exhort; to warn, caution.

def: s.f. (fr. fahm), Understanding, perception (used, like fahm, at the end of comp., e.g. zūd-fahmī, 'of quick perception').
s. f. in compos. Understanding.
(from فـﮩميدن which from Arab. فـﮩم) s. f. Understanding.

ety: v.n. fr. fahm-īdan, deriv. fr. A. fahm
def: s.f. Understanding, &c. ( = fahm, q.v.). s.m. Understanding, conception, perception, apprehension, comprehension, intellect, intelligence, sense; — (in Pers., rt. of fahm-īdan, fr. A. fahm), act. part. Understanding, apprehending, perceiving, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. suḵẖan-fahm, 'understanding a speech or discourse')
part. pass. (of فـﮩميدن) Understood, intelligent.

ety: perf. part. of fahm-īdan, fr. A. fahm
def: part. Understood, comprehended, &c.; — intelligent.

ety: fahmīda, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. ī = S. इ
def: s.f. Comprehension, apprehension, perception, &c.
s. f. Comprehension.

ety: v.n. fr. فحو 'to mean,' &c.
def: s.m. Meaning, signification, intended sense or import, tenour, drift; contents; style
more: s. m. Style, contents, signification, sense, drift, tenour, meaning. (Plur. فحاوي faḥāwī ). فحواي کلام faḥwā-e-kalām, Signification, sense, scope, meaning, spirit, contents, drift, tenour of the speech or discourse.
subs: The intended sense or import of a saying or speech, meaning or drift, &c. of a speech or composition.

def: (fa, 'then'+huwa, 'it is'), Then it is; and it is; but it is
subs: Then, or but, it is (my) wish.

ety: for A. فائدة fāʼidat, act. part. n. fr. فيد 'to accrue' (to
def: s.m. Profit, advantage, benefit, good, avail, utility, use; gain; yield; interest; value; — convalescence; — moral (of a story, &c.); result; inference; corollary
subs: (-se), To reap benefit or advantage (from); to enjoy; to utilize, to turn to account; to make the most of
(-ko), To prove beneficial (to), to benefit; to profit, avail, serve; to do good (to), to improve; to cure
adj. Profitable, beneficial, advantageous, useful; efficacious; efficient; benefited; edified; gratified
adj. & adv. Profitless, useless, bootless, unavailing; unserviceable, of no use; needless; absurd; — unprofitably; to no purpose, bootlessly, in vain, uselessly, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَایِدَه (fāyida), borrowed from Arabic فَائِدَة (fāʔida), from فَادَ (fāda).

ety: act. part. of فوق 'to become superior to,' &c.
def: adj. Superior, exce lent, surpassing; paramount; preferable.
(part. act. of فوق) adj. Superiour, excelling, exccllent.

ety: act. part. of فعل 'to do,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Doing, acting, operating; making
more: n. m. فعل doing. 1. Doer; agent; performer; operator. 2. ( Gram ). The agent, nominative, or subject. 3. ( Slang ) A sodomite. fāil-i-haqīqī . The true God. fāil-i-mukhtār . A free agent. fāil o mafūl , n. m. 1. ( Gram .) The agent and object. 2. The active and passive agents in an act of sodomy. Maë-kade meṅ go sarāsar fel-i-nā-māqūl hai, Madrasah dekhā to vāṅ bhī fāil o mafūl hai . Indulged in are all evil deeds in taverns, true, But the 'agent' and the 'object' are in High schools too. fāil o mafūl h. , v. n. To commit sodomy.

part. act. (of فعل) 1. Making, doing, operating. 2. (sub.) An agent. 3. The participle active. 4. The nominative. فاعل حقيقي fāʼiﻌl-i-ḥaḳīḳī, The true agent, God. فاعل مختار fāʼiﻌl-i-muḵẖtār, Master of oneʼs own actions.
subs: performer, operator; maker; an agent, (in Gram.) the subject (of a verbal sentence); a noun of agency
(in Gram.) The subject and the object.

ety: Gr. φιλοσοφος arabicized
def: s.m. A philosopher; — adj. & s.m. Wise, intelligent, knowing, shrewd; cunning, crafty, artful; — a cunning or artful person, a knave; an impostor.
more: n. m. Pl. falāsifā . 1. A philosopher. 2. A cunning, artful person; an imposter. Makr kā bānī, jhūṭ kā sartāj, Sunte the failsūf, dekhā āj . Wom. Shauq. Arch pretender, chief of liars I see in thee, Of subtle craft I heard before, today I see.
(Gr. Φιλοσοφος ) 1. s. m. A philosopher. 2. adj. Intelligent, knowing; ( met. in Hind.) Cunning, artful, crafty.

ety: rel. n. fr. fāʻil
def: adj. Active, operative; effective, efficient.
(for A. فاعلية, fem. of fāʻilī), adj. f. = fāʻilī, q.v. adj. Active, operative; effective, efficient.

ety: for A. فاعلية, abst. s. fr. fāʻilī
def: s.f. The state of being active; the quality or the nature of agent, or of the noun of agency; agency.

ety: fr. failasūf
def: s.f. Sophistry; hypocrisy; deception; cunning, artfulness, trickery, cheating.
more: n. f. 1. Sephistry. 2. Hypocrisy. 3. Trickery; deception; cheating.

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.f. Fire, discharge; volley
more: n. Cor. of Eng. Fire. 1. Fire; discharge; volley. 2. ( Slang ) Time or turn for the discharge of semen. fair uṛānā , v. a. ( Slang ) To have sexual intercourse with. fair k. , v. a. To fire; discharge; shoot.

ety: v.n. fr. فصل 'to separate, or divide'; 'to decide'
def: s.m. Separation, division; — deciding, decision, determination, adjudication, judicial sentence, judgment, decree; adjustment, settlement; arbitration
more: (from فصل) s. m. 1. Decision, decree, determination. 2. Division, separation. فيصل کرنا faiṣal karnā, To settle, to decide, to adjust. فيصل نامه faiṣal-nāma, s. m. A decree, an award. فيصل هونا faiṣal honā, To be decided or settled, to be adjusted.
subs: (or faiṣala) karnā or kar-denā (-kā), To settle, adjust; to determine, adjudicate, to decide, pronounce judgment (on), to judge
s.m. A judgment, a decree; an award
(or faiṣala) honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be settled, be adjusted; to be decided, be adjudicated.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic فَيْصَل (fayṣal).

ety: for فيصلة faiṣalat, n. of un. fr. faiṣal
def: s.m. Separation, division; — deciding, decision, determination, adjudication, judicial sentence, judgment, decree; adjustment, settlement; arbitration
more: n. m. فصل divided. 1. See chukāī , and hukm , 6. 2. Adjudication; arbitration; judicial determination; disposal of a case; decision. faislah-i-akhīr . Final decision or judgment. faislah az sar-i-nau . Judgment de novo. faislah jamāat-i-shūrā . Award of a council. faislah zer-i-apīl . A judgment under appeal. faislah-i-adālat . A judicial decision. faislah kā murāfah k. , v. a. To appeal from a decision. faislah murāfah-i-ūlā , Judgment of the court of first instance. faislah yā faisal k. , v. a. 1. See tasfiyā k . and chukānā , 1, 5. 2. To decide; determine judicially; judge ( tajvīz k . 2.) faislah yā faisal h. , v. n. To be decided, settled, adjudicated.
(from فصل) s. m. A decree, settlement, decision, adjudication, adjustment.
subs: (or faiṣala) karnā or kar-denā (-kā), To settle, adjust; to determine, adjudicate, to decide, pronounce judgment (on), to judge
s.m. A judgment, a decree; an award
(or faiṣala) honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be settled, be adjusted; to be decided, be adjudicated.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَیْصَلَه (faysala), from Arabic فَيْصَلَة (fayṣala). Compare Bengali ফয়সালা (foẏsala).

def: s.m. pl. (of faiṣala), Decisions, decrees, &c.
n. m. A decree, or award, faisal-nāma-i-sālisī . An arbitration award.

ety: intens. n. fr. فيض; see faiẓ
def: adj. Most bountiful, munificent, profuse, beneficent, liberal, generous; copious, abundant.
more: adj. فيض overflowed. Generous (سخي). faīyāz , n. m. A liberal or generous person ( dātā , 1).
adj. (superl. of فيض) Liberal, generous, beneficent, profuse, most bountiful.

ety: faiyāẓ, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ]
def: s.f. Munificence, liberality, generosity ( = faiẓ, q.v.)
more: n. f. Generosity ( sakhāvat ). faiyāzī k. , v. a. To be liberal.

ety: act. part. of فوز 'to attain,' &c.
def: part. adj. Attaining, acquiring, obtaining; overtaking; reaching.

ety: inf. n. of فيض 'to overflow; to abound, &c.
def: s.m. Overflowing, abundance, plenty; — beneficence munificence, liberality, bounty, bountiful kindness favour, grace; charity; good, benefit, profit
more: n. m. See سخاوت 1. ( faiyāzī ) Liberality. 2. Bounty; favor; grace. 3. Charity. faiz pahuṅchānā , v. a. To bestow favors; give alms. faiz jārī k. , v. a. To open almshouses, etc. faiz-i-ām . The public good.

(from فيض Heb. To abound, to be spread or disseminated) s. m. 1. Plenty, abundance, overflowing, profit. 2. Grace, favour, bounty. فيض عام faiẓ-i-āﻌmm, General munificence or goodness; liberal, profuse. فيضرسان faiẓ-rasān, adj. Beneficent, bountiful. فيض مدار faiẓ-madār, Centre or source of abundance or grace. فيضياب faiẓ-yāb, adj. Benefitted, blessed.
subs: adj. Bestowing favours or bounties, bountiful, beneficent, &c.; fertilizing (rain, &c.)
(-ko), To cause bounties or favours to reach, to bestow favours (on); to give alms (to)
adj. Causing abundance, &c. to reach; bountiful, &c. ( = faiẓ-baḵẖsh, q.v.)
s.m. General abundance; the public good or benefit; — adj. Diffusing or producing general abundance; liberal, profuse
s.m. Centre or source of abundance, or of grace, &c.

def: part. act. (of فوز) Overtaking, reaching, obtaining.
s. f. Munificence, beneficence.

ety: act. part. of فجر 'to decline or deviate' (from
def: adj. & s.m. Sinful; vicious, immoral; unchaste, committing adultery or fornication; — a fornicator, an adulterer; a sinner.
more: n. m. فجر transgressing. 1. A sinner. 2. A whoremonger; fornicator.
m. (part. act. of فجر) 1. sub. A fornicator, adulterer, adultress, sinner. 2. adj. Sinful, unchaste.

ety: for A. فاجرة fājirat, fem. of fājir
def: s.f. An unchaste woman, an adulteress, a harlot.
more: n. f. A loose woman ( chhināl ).
f. (part. act. of فجر) 1. sub. A fornicator, adulterer, adultress, sinner. 2. adj. Sinful, unchaste.

ety: inf. n. of فجر 'to cleave,' &c.
def: s.f. Daybreak, dawn, early morning (syn. taṛkā; bhor); — adv. In the morning, early
more: n. f. Morning ( bhor ). 1. Fajar hī fajar kyoṅ nāṅh karte ho? 2. Baṛe fajar chūlhe pe nazar . Prov. No sooner it is early dawn all eyes to kitchen fires are drawn. baṛī fajar , n. f. Early in the morning.

(orig. in cleaving, splitting or breaking; as the dawn breaks through the darkness. Hence also Heb. To be weak or wearied, fractis viribus) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) Morning, dawn of day, early. بڙي فجر baṛī fajr, Early dawn, early in the morning.
subs: s.f. & adv. Early morning; — early in the morning. (The orig. sense of 'cleaving,' both of fajr, and of taṛqā, would seem to be preserved in such expressions as the following, itnī jūtiyāṅ lageṅgī ki fajr ho-jāʼegī (or taṛqā ho-jāʼegā)).

ety: v.n. fr. فجر; see fujūr; and fājir
def: adj. Wicked, immoral, vicious; adulterous ( = fājir).
more: (from فجر) adj. Adulterous, wicked. ¶ fujūr, s. m. Wickedness, adultery.

ety: prob. corr. of A. faqd, inf. n. of فقد 'to lose,' &c.; or for A. faqq, inf. n. of فقّ = فكّ 'to separate,' &c.
def: adj. Lost, gone, flown (as colour from the face, &c.), faded; (hence) pale, pallid; blank, astonished, dismayed
more: adj. Pallid; pale. faq paṛ jānā yā ho jānā, raṅg yā muṅh faq ho jānā , v. n. To turn pale with fear, etc. Jo dekhā to sahra hai ek laq o daq, Ki Rustam jise dekh ho jāë faq . Mir Hasan.
subs: or muṅh) faq par-jānā, or faq honā or ho-jānā, v.n. (Colour) to fly from or leave (the face), to lose (colour), to turn white or pale (with fear, &c.); to look blank, &c.

ety: in A. also fūq
def: s.f. The notch of an arrow; an arrow.
more: (vulg. fāg, from فوق) s. f. The notch of an arrow, arrow.

ety: for A. فاقة fāqat; rt. فوق, of which viii افتاق iftāqa = ʻto be poor,' &c.
def: s.m. Poverty, want, need, destitution; starving, starvation, hunger; fasting, a fast (from want, not as a religious duty)
more: n. m. فوق was poor. Starvation; want. Yeh kaë fāqoṅ meṅ sīkhā thā? Prov. Saying. After how many days' starving did you learn this? (Said tauntingly to one who boasts of any accomplishment). fāqa k., yā kheṅchnā , v. n. To go without food; to fast from necessity or ill-health. fāqa-kash , n. m. One who fasts from necessity. fāqa-kashī , n. f. Fasting from necessity; endurance of hunger. Ab to fāqe-kashī kī naubat pahuṅchī hai . It's come to this that even a meal we are forced to go without. fāqa-mast , n. m. 1. A poor starveling who affects the airs of wealthy men. 2. One who is jolly in the midst of poverty. fāqa-mastī , n. f. Jollity in adversity. Fāqa-mastī ise kahte haiṅ ki gārat ho-kar Phir usī raṅg meṅ haiṅ pīr o javān-i-Dehlī . Ābid. All jovial fellows true are they who in adversity Still wear the dashing airs of young and old of fair Dilli. kaṛāke kā fāqa . A long fast (from want)…

(from فوق Heb. ) s. m. 1. Poverty, necessity, want. 2. (Pers. and in Hindī) A fast, fasting (from want, not as a religious duty). فاقه کرنا fāḳa karnā, To fast. فاقه کش fāḳa-kash, One who fasts, a faster, famished. فاقه کشي fāḳa-kash-ī, s. f. Fasting, endurance of hunger. فاقه کهـينچنا fāḳa khainchnā, or فاقون مرنا fāḳoṅ marnā, v. n. To starve, to be famished, to die with hunger. فاقه مست fāḳa-mast, One who is starving but conceals his distress.
subs: v.n. To go without food, to fast (from want, or ill-health)
adj. & s.m. Starving, famished, hungry; — a famished person; one who fasts (from necessity)
s.f. Fasting (from necessity), endurance of hunger, starvation
or fāqoṅ-marnā, To starve, to be famished, to die of hunger, perish of (necessitated and repeated) fasts
s.m. One who is starving but conceals his distress; one who is cheerful and jolly in the midst of poverty; — a poor starveling who affects the airs of a wealthy man
s.f. Cheerfulness, &c. in want or adversity
adj. & s.m. Starving, perishing of hunger; — one who is famished, a starveling
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فاقه (fāqa), from Arabic فَاقَة (fāqa).

def: (q. d. then enough. Comp. of ف Then, &c. and قط ḳat̤, Enough, which from قط ḳat̤t̤a, Chald. To cut) adv. Merely, simply, only, solely.

ety: fa, 'and'+qat̤, 'enough,' fr. قطّ 'to cut,' &c.
def: adv. Only, solely, merely, simply, and no more; — s.m. End, finis (used at the end of a document, &c.).
more: keval , adv. Alone; no more; simply ( sirf ). faqat , n. m. Finis; end. A word used at the end of a document, deed, petition, etc., to indicate that it is completed.

def: s.m.(?), A gin, trap, noose, snare; — the chase.
(part. act. of فخر) 1. adj. Excellent, precious, sumptuous, valuable: (fem. فاخره). 2. s. m. A boaster, an egotist.
(inf. n. of فخّ 'to snore'), s.m.(?), Snoring; — weakness in the legs or feet (prob. fr. P. faḵẖ).

ety: inf. n. of فخر 'to glory; to boast,' &c.
def: s.m. Glory, excellence, nobility, ornament, grace; just or proper pride; a thing to be proud of; — boasting, egotism; pride; ostentation
more: n. m. فخر he gloried. Glory; excellence; just pride, or boast. Āp kī mulāzimat bhī mujhe fakhr hai. fa/?/khr jānnā, yā samajhnā , v. n. To take a just pride in; to glory in. fakhr-i-khāndān . Pride of family or race. fakhr k. , v. n. To be justly proud of.
s. m. 1. Glory, nobility, ornament, grace. 2. Boasting, egotism, pride, ostentation.
subs: or faḵẖr samajhnā (-ko), To consider or regard as a thing to be proud of, or to glory in; to take a just pride in
(-kā), To be proud (of), to glory (in); to boast (of).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic فَخْر (faḵr, “honour, pride”).

ety: rel. n. fr. faḵẖr
def: s.f. A kind of grape so called.
more: s. f. A kind of grape so called.

ety: prob. fr. A. فخر faḵẖr+يّة — īyat
def: s.f. Boasting, vain-glory; ostentation; — adv. With just pride; boastfully.

ety: for A. فاختة faḵẖitat; said to be derived fr. faḵẖt, 'the light of the moon when it first appears,' because of its colour
def: s.f. A ringed turtle-dove; a ring-dove; a dove; — name of a colour (cf. fāḵẖtaʼī)
more: n. f. فخت A dove; turtledove; ringdove. fākhtā uṛānā , v. n. To enjoy a period of prosperity or unrestrained authority. ṭoṭrū fākhtah , The little brown dove, turtur cambayensis. chitroko fākhtah , The spotted or tiger Pigeon; turtur suratensis . serotī fākhtah , The red turtle dove; turtur humilis. kullā fākhtah , The ashy turtle dove; turtur rupicolus .

s. f. 1. A dove. 2. Name of a colour. فاخته اڙجانا fāḵẖta uṛ jānā, To be confounded, to faint. وه دن گئي جو خليل خان فا خته اڙاتي تهـي wuh din gaye jo Ḵẖalīl Ḵẖān fāḵẖta uṛāte the, The days of Khalīl Khānʼs prosperity (or splendour) are gone.
subs: To be free from care and anxiety; to enjoy a period of prosperity, or of unrestrained authority
(-kī), To be confounded, to lose (one's) senses, to swoon
s.f. The small brown dove, Turtur cambayensis
The spotted dove, the tiger-pigeon, Turtur suratensis

def: s.f. Dove-colour, fawn-colour.

ety: v.n. fr. فقه; see fiqh
def: s.m. A lawyer; a theologian.
more: (verbal adj. from فقه used substantively) A theologian.

ety: v.n. fr. فقر; see faqr
def: adj. & s.m. Poor, needy, indigent, destitute; — a poor man; a beggar; a religious mendicant; a derwish; an ascetic, a devotee
more: n. m. Pl. fuqarā . Poor; penniless; beggared. Phr. Not worth a sou , without a penny. faqīr , n. m. faqīrnī , n. f. 1. A beggar; mendicant; dervis. 1. Faqīr ko kammal hī dushālā hai . Prov. Saying. To a beggar a blanket is a shawl. 2. Kamlī oṛhne se faqīr nahīṅ hotā . Prov. The habit does not make the priest 3. Faqīr rā bā mujādla che kār? P. Prov. What has a Friar to do with fighting? 2. One who leads what is called a holy life. faqīr-dost . Friendly to dervises. faqīr k. , v. a. To reduce to beggary. faqīr kī sadā , n. Mendicants' cry. faqīr h. , v. n. 1. To adopt a hermit's life. 2. To be reduced to beggary.
(from فقر) s. m. A beggar, a dervis. adj. Poor, indigent. (Plur. فقرا or فقيران). فقير دوست faḳīr-dost, Friendly to the poor.
subs: adj. Friendly to the poor, or to derwishes
v.t. To impoverish, to reduce to beggary
v.n. To become poor, be reduced to indigence; — to become a religious mendicant, or a devotee.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَقِیر (faqīr), from Arabic فَقِير (faqīr).

ety: faqīr, q.v.+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. A poor woman; a female beggar or mendicant; a female devotee.

s.m. = faqīrāna, q.v.; — and plur. of faqīr, q.v. adj. & adv. Of or relating to a faqīr; — in the manner of a poor man; like a beggar, or a derwish; — s.m. Land, &c. bestowed on, or appropriated to the support of, faqīrs.

ety: faqīr, q.v. + Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Of or relating to a faqīr; — in the manner of a poor man; like a beggar, or a derwish; — s.m. Land, &c. bestowed on, or appropriated to the support of, faqīrs.
more: adv. In the manner of a faqīr . Mile kyoṅ-kar hame ānā terī mahfil meṅ, ejānā! Merī sūrat fagīrānā, terā darbār shāhāna . Zafar. faqīrāna , n. m. Land, etc. appropriated to the support of Faqīrs .
adv. Like a beggar or dervis.

ety: rel. n. fr. faqīr
def: adj. Of or relating to a pauper, or a beggar; pauper-like; beggarly; of or relating to a dervish, or a devotee
more: adj. Of or relating to a beggar. faqīrī laṭkā . A cheap medicine or remedy. faqīrī , n. f. 1. Poverty; beggary. 2. The life of a dervis.
1. s. f. Poverty, life of a dervis. 2. adj. Relating to or like a dervis.
subs: beggary; humility; the life of a religious mendicant, mendicity; the life of a dervish or a devotee, devoteeism.

ety: faqīr, q.v.+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. A poor woman; a female beggar or mendicant; a female devotee.

ety: inf. n. of فك 'to open'; 'to separate'
def: s.m. Unloosing, releasing, redeeming; release; redemption; liquidation; — the jaw (esp. either of the two lateral portions of the lower jaw)
subs: s.m. Redemption of a mortgage; liquidation of a debt for which property has been pledged.

ety: inf. n. of فقر 'to break (the vertebræ of the back
def: s.m. Poverty, want, need; the practice or vocation of a faqīr or derwish; a life of poverty with resignation and content; asceticism, ascetic mortification
more: n. m. Poverty; the vocation of a faqīr , or dervis; a life of poverty with resignation and content.
s. m. Poverty, the practice of a faḳīr or dervis.

def: n. m. The liquidation of a debt for which property had been pledged; redemption of a mortgage.
s.f. An omen, augury, presage; — enchantment, spell
more: n. f. An augury; a presage; bibliomancy. fāl kholnā , v. n. To practise augury or bibliomancy. fāl khulvānā , v. a. To cause events to be foretold. fāl kholne-vālā , n. m. One who foretells events by bibliomancy. fāl-i-nek ; H. achchhā shugan , n. f. A good omen.
s. f. 1. An omen, augury, presage. 2. Enchantment.
subs: (-kī), To tell (one's) fortune (by reference to his magical books, — a soothsayer); to foretell events; to practise augury, &c.
s.f. An omen taken from the first words that reach the ear
(-kī), To take an omen (from), to augur (from)
s.m. A small book of omens, &c.

ety: for A. فعلة faʻlat, and fěʻlat, n. of un. fr. فعل]
def: s.m. A (single) deed, an action.

ety: prob. fr. iflāḥ, 'prospering,' &c., inf. n. of iv of فلح 'to cleave, split,' &c.
def: s.f. Prosperity; success; state of comfort, continued enjoyment of ease; happiness; safety, security, refuge.
more: n. f. فلح prosperity. Good; profit (فايده). Mujhe satā-ke kis falāh ko pahuṅchoge?
(from فلح Heb. and Chald. To cut, to plough, to blossom as a flower: Cant. 4, 3. to prosper) s. f. Prosperity, happiness, safety, refuge.

ety: v.n. fr. فلك 'to be or become round,' &c.
def: s.m. The celestial sphere, the vault of heaven, the firmament; heaven; sky; sphere; fortune, fate
more: n. m. Sky ( āsmān ). falak-ul-aflāk . The highest heaven ( arsh ). falak-sair , n. f. ( Met .) Hemp ( bhaṅg ). Be-khudī hai, ki be-hayāī hai? Yā falak-sair tū ne khāī hai? Wom. Shauq. Or you beside yourself, or shameless are? Or you with bhaṅg intoxicated are?

(Heb. Any thing round) s. m. 1. The heavens, sky, firmament, sphere. 2. Fate, fortune. (Plur. افلاک aflāk ). فلک زده falak-zada, Stricken of fate, poor, crazy, lunatick. فلک کو خبر نـﮩوني کسيکي falak ko ḵẖabar na honī kisī ke ( lit. A thing being unknown to oneʼs stars) implies great inattention and ignorance. Mīr Aﻌbdullāh Tajarrud says:-- تجهـ رو مين لطف هي سو ملک کو خبر نـﮩين خورشيد کيا هي اسکي فلک کو خبر نـﮩين Tujh rū meṅ lut̤f hai so malak ko ḵẖabar nahīṅ; ḵẖụrshaid kyā hai? uske falak ko ḵẖabar nahīṅ, There is a charm in thy face unknown even to angels; (compared to it) what is the sun? It is unknown to his orbit or sphere. فلک الافلاک falaku-l-aflāk, The primum mobile, empyrean heaven. فلک سير falak-sair, Like the heavens in motion, rapid in motion. فلک نشان falak-nishān, Like the heavens, high or vast as the heavens.
subs: s.m. The primum mobile; the highest heaven, the empyrean
adj. Whose court is exalted as (that of) the heavens
adj. Heaven-stricken, oppressed by fate; poor, wretched; crazy, demented
adj. Like the heavens in motion, rapid in motion or action, extremely swift; — s.f. (met.) Hemp (syn. bhang).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فلک (falak), from Arabic فَلَك (falak, “[celestial] orb, [celestial] sphere”).

ety: a modern formation fr. A. falak, q.v.
def: s.f. The state of being heaven-stricken or fortune-stricken ( = falak-zadagī); evil, misfortune, adversity, misery.
more: (in Pers. and Hind. from فلک) s. f. A misfortune, adversity.

def: adj. Heaven-stricken, unfortunate, miserable, wretched.
s.f. A sling (for stones).

ety: rel. n. fr. falak
def: adj. Of or relating to the heavens; celestial, heavenly; astronomical; — of or relating to fate or fortune.
more: adj. Celestial, of the heavens, or of fortune.

def: (for A. فلاسفة falāsifat, pl. of failasūf, q.v.), s.m. pl. Philosophers.
more: n. m. Pl. of failsūf . Philosophers.

ety: also aflāt̤ūn; Gr. πλατων arabicized
def: s.m. Plato.

ety: orig. pālez
def: s.f. A melon-field.
more: (or pālez ) s. f. A field of melons.

def: s.f. See s.v. fāl. s.f. An omen, augury, presage; — enchantment, spell
subs: (-kī), To tell (one's) fortune (by reference to his magical books, — a soothsayer); to foretell events; to practise augury, &c.
s.f. An omen taken from the first words that reach the ear
(-kī), To take an omen (from), to augur (from)
s.m. A small book of omens, &c.

def: (from فلج Heb. To divide) s. m. The palsy (especially, hemiplegia).

ety: v.n. fr. falj, inf. n. of فلج 'to divide'
def: s.m. Palsy, paralysis (esp. hemiplegia)
more: q. v. The palsy; paralysis, esp. hemiplegia . fālij girnā, yā h. , v. n. To be stricken with palsy.
subs: or fālij honā, v.n. To be stricken with palsy, to be palsied or paralysed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَالَج (fālaj), from Arabic فَالَج (fālaj). First attested in c. 1665 as Middle Hindi فالج (falj).

ety: corr. of fatīla, q.v.
def: s.m. A wick; a match, a fuse; a torch; — a wick composed of paper inscribed with mystical words, by inhaling the smoke of which evil spirits are said to be expelled from those possessed; — a cord of twisted thread sewed between the two folds of the hem of a garment to strengthen it
more: n. m. Cor. of A. fatīlah . 1. A wick; fusee. Abhī patītā dīyā hai . 2. A wick made of paper on which certain mystic words are inscribed. By inhaling the smoke demons are said to be expelled from those possessed. Falītā suṅghāo . 3. A cord of twisted thread sewed between the two folds of the hem of a garment. Falitā de-ke topī sīnā. falītā denā , v. a. To light; to make a fire. falīte-dār , n. m. A matchlock ( toṛe-dār ).

(corrupt. from فتيله) s. m. 1. Match, torch, wick, fusee. 2. A wick composed of paper inscribed with mystick words, by inhaling the smoke of which demons are said to be expelled from those possessed. 3. A cord of twisted thread sewed between the two folds of the hem of a garment to strengthen it. فليته دار falīta-dār, 1. A matchlock. 2. Any thing that has a match or wick. 3. Cloth hemmed with a cord (فليته falīta, q. v.).
subs: vulg. falīte-dār, adj. & s.m. Having a falīta; — anything that has a falīta; a matchlock; cloth hemmed with a cord of twisted thread
(-meṅ), To apply a match (to), to set light (to).

def: s. (dakh.) For فاليز fālez, q. v.
s.m. A small copper coin (of the value of half a farthing); — a scale (of a fish).

ety: = phālsā, q.v.
def: s.m. The fruit of the Grewia asiatica.
more: n. m. P. pālsah . A sub-acid fruit; Grewia Asiatica ( shakri or sweet fālsa ). Ghule fālse sharbat ko! Dehlī street cry. fāl'sai, phalsaī , adj. Purple.
wety: Probably from Classical Persian فالسه

ety: for A. فلسفة falsafat; fr. failasūf, q.v.
def: s.f. Philosophy; science.

ety: rel. n. fr. falsafat
def: adj. & s.m. Philosophical; — a philosopher.
more: s. m. A philosopher. See فيلسوف.

def: adj. Of the colour of the fālsa, purple; — s.f. Purple colour.
wety: From فالسہ (“grewia, phalsa”) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. Surplus, spare; that is over and above; — unemployed.
more: adj. 1. Spare; surplus; extra (فاضل). 2. ( phālṭū ) Unemployed (hence, a cooly, Gaṛh .)
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese falto (/⁠faltu⁠/).

ety: for pālūda, perf. part. of pālūdan = bālūdan; rt. Zend. vared = S. वर्ध् (वृध्)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Strained; clear; smooth; — a kind of flummery (made of nishāsta, q.v.).
more: n. m. Cor. of P. pālūda . A kind of flummery made of nishāsta , q. v. Khunkī fōlūdah lo! Dehli street cry.
(or pālūda ) 1. adj. Smooth, clear. 2. s. m. A kind of flummery.
s. m. A sweetmeat made of sūjī, milk, sirup, khoprā, and various spices ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فالوده (fālūda), reborrowed from Arabic فَالُودَة (fālūda), from Middle Persian [script needed] (pʾlwtk' /⁠pālūdag⁠/, “starch jelly, flummery”, past participle), from Middle Persian [script needed] (pʾlwtn' /⁠pālūdan⁠/, “to strain, filter, purify”). Cognate with Bengali ফালুদা (phaluda), Gujarati ફાલુદા (phāludā) and Punjabi ਫ਼ਲੂਦਾ (falūdā).

def: n. m. ( In comp .) Color; complexion; as siyāh-fām blackish.
more: s. (dakh.) A piece of money, fanam.
wety: Borrowed from English film.

ety: for orig. فوه fūh
def: s.m. The mouth
more: (Chald. and . According to some for فوه) s. m. The mouth. (Plur. افواه) فم معده famm-i-miﻌda, The orifice of the stomach.

def: ( = wām, pām; prob. akin to S. उपमः), postpn. Like, resembling; (aff. to nouns denoting colour) of the colour of, approaching to the colour of (e.g. siyāh-fām, 'blackish'; laʻl-fām, 'ruby-coloured').
more: joined to nouns denoting colour, implies a tendency to such colour or being of such colour; as, سيه فام siyah-fām, Blackish. لعل فام laﻌl-fām, Ruby-coloured.

def: n. m. The orifice of the stomach.
The mouth of the womb.

ety: inf. n. of فنی 'to pass away, to perish,' &c.
def: s.f. Mortality, frailty, corruption, decay, perdition, destruction, death; — adj. Passed away, departed, deceased, defunct; non-existent, extinct
more: adj. فني passed away. 1. Passed away; departed; non-existent. Phr. Dead and gone; launched into eternity. Ek roz sab ko fanā hai . Prov. Saying. All must die one day. 2. Extinct; defunct. fanā , n. f. Destruction; death. fanā fillāh h. , v. n. 1. To be absorbed in the Deity. 2. See fanā ho jānā , 2. fanā k . v. a. To destroy; annihilate. fānā ho jānā , v. n. 1. To pass away; perish; vanish. 2. To die; expire.
(from فني To vanish, to perish: analog. to Heb. To turn) s. f. Mortality, frailty, perdition, destruction, death. فنا هو جانا fanā hojānā, To perish.
subs: adj. Perishable, destructible; transitory, frail, &c.; — perished, destroyed, &c.
To be absorbed in the Deity; to be lost in the contemplation of God and insensible to all else; — to die
v.n. To pass away, to vanish, perish; to cease to be, to die.

ety: prob. = band, q.v.
def: s.m.f. Artifice, deceit, fraud; falsehood; a foolish or unprofitable speech, vain words; trifles; — a point, dot; a freckle; a mole.
more: s. m. A vulgarism for فن fann, q. v.
s. Fraud, artifice, deceit: a foolish or unprofitable speech, falsehood: a point, dot, mole.

ety: rel. n. fr. fand
def: adj. False, deceitful, fraudulent.

ety: for A. فانٍ fāniṉ, act. part. of فني 'to pass away,' &c.
def: adj. Passing away, coming to an end, perishable, mortal, frail, transitory, fleeting, inconstant, evanescent
more: adj. فني passed away. 1. Transitory; fleeting; evanescent. 2. Mortal; perishable. 1. Dunyā fānī hai . Prov. All things must pass away. 2. Har chīz yahāṅ kī ānī jānī dekhī, Dunyā yeh garaz kī ham ne fānī dekhī, Jo ā-ke na jāë woh buṛhāpā dekhā, Jo jā-ke na āë woh javānī dekhī . Dabīr. All things I see here come and go, This world in short's a passing show, But old age comes yet never goes, While youth ne'er comes but ever goes. jahān-i-jānī , This transitory world.
(part. act. of فنا) adj. Frail, transitory, inconstant, perishable. جـﮩ‍ان فاني jahān-i-fānī, This transitory world.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَانِی (fānī), borrowed from Arabic فَانٍ (fānin), from فَنِيَ (faniya), from Proto-Semitic *pan-. First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi فانی (fany /⁠fānī⁠/).

ety: v.n. fr. فنّ 'to adorn,' &c.
def: s.m. Sort, species, kind; way, mode; — (in Pers. & Urdū) a craft; an art; a science; an accomplishment; skill, sagacity (syn. hunar); — art, artifice, cunning, wile, trick, ruse, manœuvre, stratagem
more: n. m. Pl. فن 1. Art; craft; accomplishment. 2. ( fan fareb ) Artifice; trick; wile; ruse ( chālākī ). Āp har fan mauld haiṅ! Prov. Saying. ( Ironic .) You are a master of every art! fann fareb , n. m. Tricks; arts. fann-farebī , adj. Crafty; intriguing; deceitful ( chālāk , 3).
s. m. 1. Science. 2. Skill, sagacity, stratagem, art, artifice. (Plur. فنون). فن فريب fann-fareb, or (dakh.) فن فتور fann-fitūr, s. m. Art and cunning. فن فر يبي fann-fareb-ī, adj. Artful, cunning.
subs: and fan-fareb, s.m. Art and cunning, arts, wiles, &c. (see fan)
adj. Artful, cunning, deceitful, crafty, wily, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَن (fan), from Arabic فَنّ (fann), from earlier Persian پند (pand).

def: adj. (dakh.) Artificial, artful ( Harris ).

ety: prob. Gr. φαρος arabicized
def: s.f. A pharos, lighthouse; a lantern; (in Urdū) a glass shade (of a candlestick, &c.)
more: n. f. A glass shade. fānūs-i-khayāl , n. m. A phantasmagoria. Āne lage baiṭhe baiṭhe chakkar, Fānūs-i-khayāl ban gayā ghar . Nasīm.

s. f. A shade (to keep the wind from a candle), a lantern, a pharos, lighthouse. فانوس خيال fānūs-i-ḵẖiyāl, or فانوس خيالي fānūs-i-ḵẖiyāl-ī, A lantern which revolves by the smoke of the candle within, and has on the sides of it figures of various animals.
subs: or fānūs-ě-gardān, or ćarḵẖī fānūs, A Chinese lantern; a lantern into which a light framework with pictures attached thereto is inserted (the framework and pictures are caused to revolve by the smoke of the candle); a phantasmagoria; a magic lantern.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فَانُوس (fānūs, “lantern”), itself from Arabic فَانُوس (fānūs, “lantern”).

def: s.m. A rat; a mouse (syn. ćūhā).

ety: inf. n. of فرع 'to overtop; to surpass,' &c.
def: s.f. The upper or uppermost part (of anything); — a branch (of a tree or plant, &c.), a bough; the head of a branch
more: n. f. A branch ( shākh ). fur'ū , n. m. Pl. of farā . Preliminary doctrines of a faith.
s. f. A bough, a branch (of a tree, particularly the top branches). (Plur. فُروع).
subs: subdivision; a derivative (syn. shāḵẖ); a fundamental institute or doctrine, &c.

ety: gen. referred to A. furr, rt. فرّ 'to flee'; but prob. for P. par; or akin to S. sphaṭ, or sphur
def: s.m. Light, splendour, brilliancy, lustre, glory; beauty, comeliness, elegance, grace, ornament; magnificence, glory, power, dignity, dominion, pomp, state.
more: (Arab. فرّ furr, The best and handsomest) s. m. State, pomp, dignity, splendour, glory, lustre, beauty, grace.

ety: inf., or v.n. of فرغ 'to be free' (from
def: s.m. Freedom (from business, &c.), disengagement, &c. ( = farāg̠at, q.v.)
more: (from فرغ) s. m. Cessation, disengagement (from any thing on finishing it), competency, completion, leisure, repose, ease, freedom from care.
subs: (-se), To cease (from), to have finished or done (with), &c.

ety: for A. فراغة farāgat, fr. farāg̠, q.v.
def: s.f. Freedom (from business, &c.), cessation (from work, &c.), finishing and ceasing (from), disengagedness, leisure, rest, repose; freedom from care or anxiety, ease, convenience, comfort, tranquillity, happiness; easy circumstances, competency, affluence, abundance; — easing oneself, a call of nature
more: n. f. فرغ free. See chhuṭkārā , (2). 1. Cessation from labor; respite; breathing time; interval of rest. 2. Freedom from care; ease; comfort; convenience. 3. Competency; sufficiency. 4. Call of nature ( paikhānā ). farāgat phirnā, yā jānā , v. a. To go to the privy. farāgat se, bā-farāgat , adv. Leisurely; easily. farāgat lagnā , v. n. To be taken short. farāgat meṅ , adv. At one's convenience, ease, or leisure. farāgat h., yā pānā, fārig h. , v. n. 1. To be free or rid of; to have done with. 2. To have leisure.

(from فرغ) s. f. 1. Cessation, disengagement (from any thing on finishing it), finishing and ceasing (from), rest, freedom from care, leisure, repose. 2. Abundance, affluence, ease, happiness, competency. فراغت کرنا farāg̠ẖat karnā, To finish and cease (from).
subs: (-se), To obtain freedom (from), to have finished (with), have done (with); to get leisure
v.n. To go to ease oneself, to go to the rear
adv. Leisurely, at ease, easily, comfortably
(-se), To finish and cease (from); to make an end (of)
(-ko), To have a call of nature
adv. At (one's) leisure, or convenience, or ease

ety: inf. n. of فرح 'to rejoice, be joyful, &c.
def: adj. Joyful, joyous, glad, pleased, cheerful, blithe; satisfied, &c.; — s.f. Joy, mirth, gladness, cheerfulness, joyousness, happiness
more: (Heb. To bud, to blossom, to prosper) 1. adj. Joyful, pleased, satisfied, cheerful. 2. s. Joy, gladness. فرح بخش faraḥ-baḵẖsh, adj. Imparting gladness.

ety: farā+ham, fr. Zend fra+ham = S. प्र+सम्
def: adj. & s.m. Brought together, collected, gathered, accumulated; assembled; embodied; — collection; conjunction; contraction; — adv. Together
more: (comp. of فرا prep. and هم Together) 1. s. m. Collection. 2. Contraction. 3. adj. Collected, gathered. فراهم کرنا farāham karnā, To accumulate, to gather.
subs: v.t. To collect, gather together, accumulate, amass.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فراهم (farāham).

def: adv. See ikhaṭṭā , 1. 1. Brought together; gathered; accumulated; convoked; congregated. 2. Embodied. farāham k. , v. a. To amass ( ikhaṭṭā k .). farāh'imī , n. f. Collecting or assembling together. farāhimī-i-chandah . Collecting subscriptions. farāhimī-i-sipāh . Collecting an army; mobilization. farāhimī-i-tulabā . Collecting students.
s.f. A gathering together, collecting, assembling; collection.
